lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: Which do I like?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, y'know for dating, general merriment, taking back to your windmill...

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: Well, if I had to chose right now, which by the way I find really weird, I would have to say, Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait a minute! Wait a minute! She obviously didn’t understand the question.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you don’t you have Captain Hook explain it to her.

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: I’m sorry, Joey, that is my chose.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You hear that! That is her chose, mister I’ll let you have her! I win! You suck! I rule all! A mini-wave in celebration of me!!

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: I’m now thinking I would like to change my answer to, no one.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wh-what?

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: I now find you shallow and um, a dork. All right, bye.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nice going. You just saved yourself a couple months of sex.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what, it doesn’t matter, ‘cause she picked me. Me! From now on I get the dates and you have to stay home on Saturday nights watching Ready, Set, Cook!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Save the breakthroughs for therapy, okay. The clock is ticking. We have no time, and we are losing, we are losing to girls.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We’re not gonna lose to girls.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! It’s 42-21!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This sucks, I was just up by that much!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are we playing football or what? Come on you hairy-backed Marries.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We have to do this. We are playing for women everywhere. Okay, just think about every lousy date that you ever had, okay, every guy who kept on the TV while you’re making out...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! You dated someone with a glass eye too?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on, okay, come on this is for all womankind. Let’s kill ‘um!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!!! Kill ‘um!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, no, well I want to kill them to, but their boys, y'know how are we gonna beat three boys?

scenes.scene_description: Ross makes a ‘Yeah. Right.’ sound.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, we still have a minute and a half to go, and we’re down by two points. Two points.... Phoebe you do a button-hook again. Rachel, you go long.
lines.stage_direction: she gets interrupted by the guys, who are doing a slow-motion high five.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Come on! Don’t make me go long. Use me. They never cover me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, there’s a reason.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: God, I’m not lame, okay. I can do something. I can throw, would you let me throw, come on this is my game too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on Mon, let her throw the ball.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right Rachel, you sweep behind, I’ll pitch it to you, you throw it down field to Phoebe. All right. Break.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you! Break!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thirty-two! Seventy-one! Hike!

scenes.scene_description: Another football sequence follows

lines.speaker: The Guys
lines.text: One-Mississippi! Two-Mississippi! Three-Mississippi!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe snaps the ball to Monica, who pitches back to Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m so sorry! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! I’m not okay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m sorry, they were just all coming at me, and I didn’t know what to do.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thirty seconds left on the timer!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, okay, so we get to take that stupid troll thing home!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on! Come on! Hurry! We’re running out of time! Huddle up!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Oooh! Oh, this is our last huddle, yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, Phoebe get open. Rachel, go long.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Break!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I got a touchdown! We did it!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey-hey-hey Rachel, funny thing. Actually, the ah, end zone starts at that pole, so you’re five feet short, so we win!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait-wait-wait-wait! So, explain something to me though, if, if nobody tagged Rachel, then isn’t the play still going.

scenes.scene_description: They all rush toward Rachel, who panics and runs away. She runs out of the park and up along the fence, she then comes back into the park and runs past Monica, as she gets to Monica, she throws the ball at Monica, and it hits her in the eye.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let go! Let go!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Let go! I’m a tiny little woman!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Guys! Guys! Come on! It’s Thanksgiving, it’s not important who wins or loses. The important thing is, the Dutch girl picked me! Me! Not you! Holland loves Chandler! Thank you, Amsterdam! Good night!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ow!!

scenes.scene_description: they all start to dive for the ball and Monica and Ross grab it at the same time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We should defiantly play football more often. Maybe there’s a like league we could join or something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Isn’t there a national football league.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes. Yes, there is, they play on Sundays and Monday nights.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh shoot! I work Monday nights.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, this stuffing is amazing. Do you think we should bring them some?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: When they’re hungry enough, they’ll come in.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Rachel, Phoebe, Chandler, and Joey are eating Thanksgiving dinner.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Let go!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! You let go!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How come it’s always us left in the field holding the ball?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don’t know. I guess the other people just don’t care enough.

scenes.scene_description: The park, it’s dark outside and Monica and Ross are still fighting over the ball.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! It’s starting to snow.

scenes.scene_description: It starts snowing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Gimme the this!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Let go!

scenes.scene_description: They both look up, and watch it start to snow. Then they both start fighting for the ball again.

title: The One Where Rachel Quits

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Eh..., I don’t, I don’t know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, as old as he is in dog years, do you think Snoopy should still be allowed to fly this thing?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Do you remember when you first came here, how you spent two weeks getting trained by another waitress?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, sure! Do you need me to train somebody new?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Good one. Actually, ah, Terry wants you to take the training again, whenever.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Eh, do you believe that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah?

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Chandler and Rachel are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: So that’s two boxes of the Holiday Macaroons. On behalf of the Brown Birds of America, I salute you.
lines.stage_direction: Does the Brown Bird salute, she blows on a bird call, then holds her hand, palm facing out, next to her face, and then waves it like a bird flapping it’s wings.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just admit it Chandler, you have no backhand.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Excuse me little one, I have a very solid backhand.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Shielding your face and shrieking like a girl... is not a backhand.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I was shrieking... like a Marine.

scenes.scene_description: The hallway of Ross’s building, there is a Brown Bird girl selling cookies, as Ross and Chandler come up the stairs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right here. Watch me execute the three ‘P’s of championship play. Power. Precision. and penache.
lines.stage_direction: swings the racquet swings the racquet. does a backswing and hits Sarah who’s started up the stairs, knocking her down, they both watch in horror.

scenes.scene_description: they both start up the stairs.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You broke a little girl’s leg?!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know. I feel horrible. Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Says here that a muppet got whacked on Seasame Street last night. Where exactly were around ten-ish?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I’m gonna go see her. I want to bring her something, what do you think she’ll like?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Maybe a Hello Kitty doll, the ability to walk...

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, the gang’s all there discussing the incident.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m gonna get back to retraining.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, see you guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look out kids, he’s coming!
lines.stage_direction: Ross continues to leave with his head down in shame.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And I gotta go sell some Christmas trees.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Have fun. Oh wait, no, don’t! I forgot I am totally against that now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? Me having a job?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, I am against innocent trees being cut down in their prime, and their, their corpses grotesquely dressed in like tinsel and twinkly lights. Hey, how do you sleep at night?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I’m pretty tired from lugging the trees around all day. Hey, Phoebe listen, you got this all wrong. Those trees were born to be Christmas trees, their fulfilling their life purpose, by, by making people happy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?

scenes.scene_description: Rachel starts to laugh, and Ross notices her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes. Yes, and ah, ah, the trees are happy too, because for most of them, it’s the only chance to see New York.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: ...and after you’ve delivered the drinks, you take the empty tray....

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gunther, Gunther, please, I’ve worked here for two and a half years, I know the empty trays go over there.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: What if you put them here.
lines.stage_direction: sets the empty tray on another stack of empty trays on the back counter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh. Well, y'know that’s actually a really good idea, because that way they’ll be closer to the mugs. Y'know what, you should have the other waitresses do that too.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: They already do. That’s why they call it the ‘tray spot.’

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gee, I always heard them talk about that, I just always thought that it was a club they went to. Oh God, I’m, I’m sorry.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: It’s all right. Sweetheart.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe turns and looks at Monica, while Joey frantically motions to Chandler to help him out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, this must be kinda neat for ya, huh? I mean, your Dad tells me that you get a couple of days off school, and you, you ah, don’t have to sell those cookies anymore.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Well, I kinda wanted to sell the cookies. The girl who sells the most wins a trip to Spacecamp, and gets to sit in a real space shuttle.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow, you ah, you really like all this space stuff, huh?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Yeah. My Dad says if I spend as much time helping him clean apartments, as I do daydreaming about outer space, he’d be able to afford a trip to the Taj Mahal.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think you would have to clean a whole lot of apartments to go all the way to India.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: No. The one in Atlantic City, Dad loves the slots. He says he’s gonna double the college money my Grandma left me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh. Well, good luck to Dad. Say, how many more boxes would you have to sell in order to win?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: The girl who won last year sold four hundred and seventy-five.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: So far, I’ve sold seventy-five.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Four hundred, huh? Well, that sounds do-able. How much are the boxes?
lines.stage_direction: starts to get out his wallet

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Five dollars a box.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And what is second prize?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: A ten speed bike. But, I’d rather have something my Dad couldn’t sell.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, that makes sense.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Could you do me one favor, if it’s not too much trouble?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, Sarah, anything.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Could you pull open the curtains for me? The astronauts from the space shuttle are gonna be on the news, and since we don’t have a TV, the lady across the alley said she’d push hers up to a window, so I could watch it.

scenes.scene_description: Sarah’s bedroom, her room is decorated with a space motif.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi, I’m selling Brown Bird cookies.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: You’re no Brown Bird, I can see you through my peephole.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, hi, I’m, I’m an honorary Brown Bird

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: What does that mean?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, well, it means that I can sell cookies, but I’m not invited to sleep-overs.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I can dial 9-1-1 at the touch of a button, y'know. Now, go away!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, please, please, um, it’s for a poor little girl who wants to go to Spacecamp more than anything in the world.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I’m pressing, a policeman is on his way.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay! I’m going. I’m going.
lines.stage_direction: goes across the hall to knock on another door.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I can still see you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. What, what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I-I thought a lot about what you said, and um, I realilized duh, all right maybe I was a little judgmental. Yeah, oh, but oh...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look now, Phoebe remember, hey, their just fulfilling their Christmas....

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Destiny.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Yikes! That one doesn’t look very fulfilled.
lines.stage_direction: One of Joey’s co-workers, walks by with a dead tree.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, that’s, that’s ah, one of the old ones, he’s just taking it to the back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You keep the old ones in the back, that is so ageist.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well we have to make room for the fresh ones.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, what happens to the old guys?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, they go into the chipper.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why, do I have a feeling that’s not as happy as it sounds? No! Nooooo!!!
lines.stage_direction: Joey points out one going into the chipper to her, as this haunty, demonic music starts to play in the background she winces in horror and hides her face against Joey’s shoulder, as she sees the tree spit out from the chipper.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Hey!!
lines.stage_direction: to the guy operating the chipper makes the ‘cut it’ motion with his hands

scenes.scene_description: Joey’s work, selling Christmas trees.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...and these come in the shapes of your favourite Christmas characters, Santa, Rudolph, and Baby Jesus.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I’ll take a box of the cream filled Jesus’s.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute, one box! Come on, I’m trying to send a little girl to Spacecamp, I’m putting you down for five boxes. Chandler, what about you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahh, do you have any coconut flavoured deities?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, but ah, there’s coconut in the Hanukkah Menoreoes. I tell you what, I’ll put you down for eight boxes, one for each night.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mon?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I’ll take one box of the mint treasures, just one, and that’s it. I-I started gaining weight after I joined the Brown Birds. Remember, how Dad bought all my boxes and I ate them all?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, no Mon, Dad had to buy everyone of your boxes because you ate them all. But ah, y'know I’m sure that’s not gonna happen this time, why don’t I put you down for three of the mint treasures and just a couple of the Rudolph’s.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, come on, now you know you want ‘em.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don’t, don’t, don’t, don’t, don’t do this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’ll tell you what Mon, I’ll give you the first box for free.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God! I gotta go!
lines.stage_direction: she reaches out for it and stops runs out

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on! All the cool kids are eating ‘em!

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: And when you have a second later, I wanna show you why we don’t just trap spiders under coffee mugs and leave them there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m training to be better at a job that I hate, my life officially sucks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look Rach, wasn’t this supposed to a temporary thing? I thought you wanted to do fashion stuff?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, yeah! I’m still pursuing that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How... exactly are you pursuing that? Y'know other than sending out resumes like what, two years ago?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I’m also sending out.... good thoughts.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: If you ask me, as long as you got this job, you’ve got nothing pushing you to get another one. You need the fear.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: The fear?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He’s right, if you quit this job, you then have motivation to go after a job you really want.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well then how come you’re still at a job that you hate, I mean why don’t you quit and get ‘the fear’?

scenes.scene_description: Chandler mouths ‘Okay.’

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because, I’m too afraid.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know, I mean I would give anything to work for a designer, y'know, or a buyer.... Oh, I just don’t want to be 30 and still work here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, that’d be much worse than being 28, and still working here.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Remind me to review with you which pot is decaf and which is regular.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can’t I just look at the handles on them?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: You would think.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, fine. Gunther, y'know what, I am a terrible waitress, do you know why I’m a terrible waitress? Because, I don’t care. I don’t care. I don’t care which pot is regular and which pot is decaf, I don’t care where the tray spot is, I just don’t care, this is not what I want to do. So I don’t think I should do it anymore. I’m gonna give you my weeks notice.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gunther, I quit.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Does this mean we’re gonna have to start paying for coffee?
lines.stage_direction: to Joey Joey shrugs his shoulders.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey both laugh

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I spelled out boobies.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, but me down for another box of the mint treasures, okay. Where, where are the mint treasures?
lines.stage_direction: comes up and starts looking through Ross’s cookie supply

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, we’re out. I sold them all.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica, I’m cutting you off.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. No, just, just, just a couple more boxes. It-it-it’s no big deal, all right, I’m-I’m cool. You gotta help me out with a couple more boxes!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mon, look at yourself. You have cookie on your neck.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God!
lines.stage_direction: covers her neck runs to the bathroom

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, how many have you sold so far?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Check this out. Five hundred and seventeen boxes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God, how did you do that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, the other night I was leaving the museum just as ‘Laser Floyd’ was letting out of the planetarium, without even trying I sold 50 boxes! That’s when it occurred to me, the key to my success, ‘the munchies.’ So I ah, started hitting the NYU dorms around midnight. I am selling cookies by the case. They

lines.speaker: call me
lines.text: 'Cookie Dude!'

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, stop what you’re doing, I need envelope stuffers, I need stamp lickers.....

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well hey, who did these resumes for ya?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Me! On my computer.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well you sure used a large font.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Eh, yeah, well ah, waitress at a coffee shop and cheer squad co-captain only took up so much room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey-hey-hey that’s funny! Your funny Chandler! Your a funny guy! You wanna know what else is really funny?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Something else I might have said?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know, I don’t know, weren’t you the guy that told me to quit my job when I had absolutely nothing else to do. Ha! Ha! Ha! Ha! Ha!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sweetie, calm down, it’s gonna be okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it’s not gonna be okay Ross, tomorrow is my last day, and I don’t have a lead. Okay, y'know what, I’m just gonna, I’m just gonna call Gunther and I’m gonna tell him, I’m not quitting.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You-you-you don’t wanna give into the fear.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You and your stupid fear. I hate your fear. I would like to take you and your fear....

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! I got great news!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Run, Joey! Run for your life!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? Rachel, listen, have you ever heard of Fortunata Fashions?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well my old man is doing a plumbing job down there and he heard they have an opening. So, you want me to see if I can get you an interview?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Yes, I would love that, oh, that is soo sweet, Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Not a problem.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And now for the great news.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, that wasn’t the great news?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Only if you think it’s better than this... snow-in-a-can!! I got it at work. Mon, you want me to decorate the window, give it a kind of Christmas lookie.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Christmas cookie?

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Chandler is entering numbers on a calculator as Ross reads off how much he’s sold.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, and ah, this one here is a Douglas Fir, now it’s a little more money, but you get a nicer smell.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Looks good. I’ll take it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait, wait, wait, wait, wait! No, no, you don’t want that one. No, you can have this cool brown one.
lines.stage_direction: running up carrying a tree points to the almost dead tree she has

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: It’s-it’s-it’s almost dead!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay but that’s why you have to buy it, so it can fulfil it’s Christmas destiny, otherwise there gonna throw it into the chipper. Tell him, Joey

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, the ah, trees that don’t fulfil their Christmas destiny are thrown in the chipper.

lines.speaker: Guy
